title: The total change of a canonical KdV update is the negative canonical boundary flux mismatch

source.notes: Generated from checked Lean ground truth; source repository withheld during public release.

informal.statement: For the canonical KdV update, the total change equals minus scale times the canonical KdV boundary flux mismatch.
assumptions: all scalar parameters are real numbers
u is a real-valued grid state on natural-number indices
n and i are natural-number grid or range indices as used in the theorem statement
the KdV scaffold definitions are those in LeanNumerics.FiniteDifference.Kdv
informal.expected_conclusion: The canonical total change is exactly the negative scaled boundary flux mismatch.

declarations.name: totalChange_kdvCanonicalUpdate
declarations.statement: theorem totalChange_kdvCanonicalUpdate (scale : Real) (u : Nat -> Real) (n : Nat) : totalOn (fun i => kdvCanonicalUpdate scale u i) n - totalOn u n = -scale * (kdvCanonicalFlux u n - kdvCanonicalFlux u 0)

tasks.input: Translate this KdV conservative scaffold theorem into Lean 4: For the canonical KdV update, the total change equals minus scale times the canonical KdV boundary flux mismatch.
